info.name: FHIR R4 Foundations — fhir.sk
info.description: HL7 FHIR R4 Foundations collection for the FHIR.sk Interoperability Lab. Covers CRUD operations on Patient, Observation, Bundle transactions, and CapabilityStatement. Uses synthetic data only.

request.description: Retrieve the server's CapabilityStatement. This describes what the FHIR server supports: resources, interactions, search parameters, and FHIR version. Always start here to understand what a FHIR server can do.

Create a new synthetic Patient resource. FHIR uses POST to create a resource. The server may assign its own ID, or accept the client-provided ID. Returns 201 Created on success with a Location header pointing to the new resource.
Read a specific Patient by FHIR resource ID. The URL pattern is [base]/[ResourceType]/[id]. Returns 200 OK with the full Patient resource, or 404 Not Found if the patient does not exist.

query.description: Search by patient family name (partial match supported)

request.description: Search for Patients by name. FHIR search returns a Bundle of type 'searchset'. The bundle contains entries for each matching Patient. The 'name' search parameter matches against both family and given names.
Search for a Patient by their identifier (system|value format). This is how you look up a patient by their national ID or hospital number in real systems.

Update a Patient using HTTP PUT. In FHIR, PUT replaces the entire resource (full update). The resource ID in the URL must match the id in the body. FHIR maintains version history — you can retrieve previous versions using the _history endpoint.
Delete a Patient resource. In FHIR, DELETE performs a 'soft delete' by default — the resource is marked as deleted but version history is preserved. Returns 200 OK or 204 No Content on success.

Create a body weight Observation using LOINC code 29463-7. The value uses UCUM units (kg). The subject references the synthetic Patient. LOINC codes are the standard for lab and clinical observations in FHIR.
Search for all Observations associated with the synthetic Patient. The 'patient' search parameter filters by subject reference.

POST a transaction Bundle to the FHIR server base URL. A transaction Bundle processes all entries atomically — if any entry fails, all entries are rolled back. This Bundle creates a Patient and an Observation together. Internal references using urn:uuid are resolved by the server.

Retrieve the full version history of a Patient resource. FHIR maintains a version history for all resources. Every update creates a new version. The _history interaction returns a Bundle of type 'history' with all versions.
Retrieve a specific version of a Patient resource using the vread interaction. Version ID is typically a sequential number. Useful for auditing and understanding what changed between versions.
